header.headline: IR Dataflow Stitching
header.subtitle: Stitching keeps producer-consumer relationships explicit so codegen can preserve the intended graph semantics.

producers.title: Producer Ops
producers.body: Early graph operators define the upstream values and dependencies.

stitch.title: Stitch Layer
stitch.body: The IR reifies the handoff points so later passes can preserve dataflow structure.

kernels.title: Kernel Groups
kernels.body: Stitched values are lowered into kernel-level work units without losing origin.

artifacts.title: IR Artifacts
artifacts.body: The final artifacts keep lineage visible to debugging and runtime tooling.

outcome.title: Visible Lineage
outcome.body: A healthy stitched architecture keeps provenance visible from graph to kernel.

footer.note: Dataflow stitching is an architecture problem because lineage must survive lowering.
